app.name: ChatFlick by coreXmanzoid
app.description: A Flask-based full-stack social microblogging web application with dual desktop and mobile UIs, email and Google OAuth authentication, follower relationships, post creation and engagement, threaded comments, in-app and Firebase push notifications, profile management with Cloudinary image hosting, granular settings and privacy controls, an admin dashboard, a Groq-powered AI assistant (Manzoid-AI), a pricing/plans page, and account data export.

stack.backend: Flask (Python), SQLAlchemy ORM, Flask-Login, Authlib (Google OAuth), itsdangerous (signed tokens)
stack.frontend: Jinja2 templates, Bootstrap 5, jQuery, Bootstrap Icons, Vanilla JS
stack.push_notifications: Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) via firebase-admin SDK

user_status_levels.UNVERIFIED: Default status after registration. Severely restricted: cannot follow, comment, upload profile photos, or receive mention notifications.
user_status_levels.VERIFIED: Email confirmed. Full platform access. Required for all social interactions.
user_status_levels.PRO: Granted by admin only (no billing flow implemented yet). Treated as verified across the platform. Shows a verified badge on profile.
user_status_levels.DEACTIVED: User self-deactivated via danger zone. Data preserved. Reactivated by logging back in.
user_status_levels.BLOCKED: Set by admin (manually or automatically after 3 warnings). Prevents posting, commenting, following, and AI usage. Reversible by admin.
user_status_levels.PENDING_PRO: Internal admin workflow state — user has requested Pro but it has not been approved yet.

decorators.verified_user: Checks current_user.status == 'VERIFIED' exactly. PRO and Verified (Google) statuses do NOT pass this guard. Returns 403 on failure.
decorators.pro_user: Checks current_user.status == 'PRO'. Defined but NOT actively applied to any routes currently.
decorators.only_admin: Checks current_user.username == 'coreXmanzoid'. Hardcoded username-based admin check.

authentication.description: Handles signup, credential login, Google OAuth login, email verification, password reset, and logout.

routes.GET/POST /signup/<int:st>: st=0 renders signup form. st=1 (POST JSON) checks username availability. st=0 POST processes signup form with CAPTCHA.
routes.GET/POST /login: Renders login form or processes credentials. Requires CAPTCHA.
routes.GET /google/authorized: Google OAuth callback. Creates or retrieves user. Auto-sets status to 'VERIFIED'.
routes.GET/POST /reset-password: GET renders reset form. POST (JSON) validates CAPTCHA, sends reset link email if email exists.
routes.GET/POST /reset-password/<token>: GET renders new-password form. POST (JSON) validates token and resets password.
routes.GET /verify-email/<token>: Verifies email token and sets status to 'VERIFIED'. Token expires after 20 minutes.
routes.POST /email-verification: Sends verification email to current logged-in user.
routes.GET /logout/<int:st>: st=1 redirects to /home, st=2 (switch account) redirects to /login.

services.AuthService.create_user: Hashes password with pbkdf2:sha256 salt_length=8. Sets status to UNVERIFIED.
services.GoogleAuthService.get_or_create_user: Looks up by email. If new, builds unique username from Google given_name, sets status='VERIFIED', generates random password.
services.CaptchaService.verify_turnstile: Posts to Cloudflare /turnstile/v0/siteverify with secret key and IP.

edge_cases: Password reset returns same JSON response whether email exists or not (anti-enumeration).
Google accounts get status='VERIFIED' (title-case) not 'VERIFIED' (uppercase). The verified_user decorator checks for exact uppercase 'VERIFIED' so Google users cannot pass it.
Username availability check returns status='abondonded' (misspelled) when username exists.
Logout route behavior is determined by integer path parameter, not session policy.
CAPTCHA is required on signup, login, and password-reset-request flows.

membership_and_verification.description: Controls feature gating based on account status tiers.

behavior.home_page: Shows email verification overlay for UNVERIFIED users. PRO users are treated as verified on the home page (status == 'VERIFIED' or status == 'PRO').
behavior.verified_user_decorator: Only accepts exact string 'VERIFIED'. PRO, Verified (Google's title-case), and all other statuses fail with 403.
behavior.protected_features: Follow/unfollow, comments, profile image upload, and mention notifications all require @verified_user.
behavior.pro_user_decorator: Defined in decorators.py but applied to zero routes currently.
behavior.pricing_page: Exists at /pricing with Free/$0, Pro/$12/mo, Enterprise/$49/mo plans. No backend billing or subscription logic implemented.

posts.description: Full post lifecycle: creation, editing (3-minute window), deletion, hashtag extraction, mention parsing.

routes.POST /managePosts/1: Create post. Validates user_id matches current_user.id. Rejects empty content. Extracts hashtags (removes from stored content). Parses @mentions.
routes.POST /managePosts/2: Edit post. Checks can_modify (owner + within 3 minutes). Does NOT re-parse hashtags or mentions.
routes.POST /managePosts/3: Delete post. Checks can_modify. Cascade-deletes all associated comments.
routes.GET /showPosts/<int:state>/<int:id>: state=0 random, state=1 profile posts, state=2 following feed (limit 10), state=3 liked posts (limit 10), state=4 reposted posts.
routes.POST /PostAction/<int:state>: state=1 like/unlike, state=2 repost/undo, state=3 like/unlike comment, state=4 share (increments counter).
routes.POST /send-mention-notifications: Sends mention notifications for a post or comment. Requires verified_user.

models.Post: id, content, user_id (FK), hashtags (JSON list), mentions (JSON list of {user_id, username}), timestamp (DateTime UTC), likes (int), comments (int), reposts (int), shares (int). Cascade deletes Comments.
models.Comments: id, content, post_id (FK), user_id (FK), mentions (JSON), likes (int). No timestamp field.

services.PostService.create_post: Splits by newlines, strips #words per line, stores clean content. Hashtags stored as list of raw '#word' strings.
services.PostService.can_modify: Checks post.user.id == user_id AND datetime.now(UTC) - post.timestamp < timedelta(minutes=3). Normalizes naive timestamps to UTC.
services.PostActionService.like_post: Checks post_id not in current_user.liked_posts before incrementing. Floors at 0 on unlike.
services.PostActionService.repost_post: Same uniqueness pattern as likes via current_user.reposted_posts JSON array.
services.PostActionService.like_comment: No per-user uniqueness check. Simply increments/decrements.
services.PostActionService.share_post: No per-user uniqueness. Simply increments shares counter.

edge_cases: Backend does NOT enforce the 180-character frontend limit on posts.
Backend does NOT enforce the 130-character frontend limit on comments.
Editing does not re-parse hashtags or mentions.
Missing posts during edit/delete return non-standard string status messages instead of HTTP error codes.
Self-like, self-repost, self-share are not blocked at the backend.
Comment likes are not uniquely tracked — same user can toggle repeatedly.
Share counter has no per-user tracking.
Milestone notifications (100, 1K, 10K likes/reposts/comments) are triggered client-side in home.js, not server-side.

feed.description: Builds post collections for various feed types, rendered via posts.html template.

feed_types.state=0 (random): FeedService.random_posts() — ORDER BY RANDOM() LIMIT 10. Not personalized.
feed_types.state=1 (profile): FeedService.profile_posts(user_id) — all posts by user, ordered newest first. No limit.
feed_types.state=2 (following): FeedService.following_posts(user_id) — posts from followed accounts, newest first, LIMIT 10.
feed_types.state=3 (liked): FeedService.liked_posts(user_id) — posts whose IDs are in user.liked_posts JSON array, LIMIT 10.
feed_types.state=4 (reposted): FeedService.reposted_posts(user_id) — returns (posts, username) tuple. username is used in template to show 'X reposted' label.

edge_cases: Following feed returns empty list when user follows nobody.
Liked and reposted feeds return empty when tracking arrays are empty.
Random feed is not personalized and may show the user's own posts.
Reposted feed has no LIMIT unlike liked posts.

comments.description: Threaded comment system on posts. Single level (no nested replies).

routes.GET /comments/<int:post_id>: Returns rendered comments.html with post and up to 15 comments sorted by likes DESC.
routes.POST /comments/<int:post_id> (state=1): Creates comment. Parses mentions. Increments post.comments counter. Returns {mentions, comment_id, comments_count}.

services.CommentService.add_comment: Creates Comments record linked to current_user. Parses mentions. Increments post.comments.
services.CommentService.get_comments: Returns all comments for post ordered by likes DESC. Route slices to [:15].

edge_cases: Adding comments requires @verified_user (exact 'VERIFIED' status).
Backend does not reject empty comment content before persisting.
Comment list is capped to 15 by slicing in the route, not in the query.
Comments model has no timestamp — cannot sort by recency.
Comment likes have no per-user tracking.

social_graph.description: Follow and unfollow relationships. Follower/following counts on profiles.

routes.POST /follows/<int:id>/<int:st>: st=1 follow, st=2 unfollow. Returns {status, follower_id, follower_name, followersCount}. Requires @verified_user.
routes.GET /exploreAccounts/<int:id>/<string:st: state='random' returns 10 random accounts. state='following' returns accounts this user follows. state='followers' returns accounts following this user. Any other string is treated as a username search query.

models.Follow: id, follower_id (FK to user_data), following_id (FK to user_data). Bidirectional relationships on UserData.

services.FollowService.follow_user: Creates Follow(follower_id=current_user.id, following_id=user_id). No duplicate prevention.
services.FollowService.unfollow_user: Queries for existing Follow and deletes it. Silent success if not found.
services.AccountService.search_accounts: WHERE username CONTAINS query (lowercase), GROUP BY id, ORDER BY follower count DESC. If results > 10, random.sample(10) returned.

edge_cases: No prevention for self-following.
No unique constraint preventing duplicate follow rows.
Search only matches usernames, not names, bios, or other fields.
Explore route falls back to search logic for any unknown non-special state string.

profile.description: User profile display and profile picture upload.

routes.GET /profile/<int:id>: id=0 returns newPost.html template (new post composer). Otherwise returns profile.html with user data and is_following flag.
routes.POST /update-profile/<int:id>: Uploads profile image to Cloudinary. Requires login, @verified_user, file present, non-empty filename, user exists, current_user.id == id.

profile_template_features: Shows profile image, name, PRO badge (bi-patch-check-fill) if status==PRO
Shows username, birth date (if show_birthdate setting), bio (if show_bio setting), website link
Shows Follow/Following/Edit Profile button based on relationship
Shows follower and following counts (if show_status setting)
ProfileOptions tabs: Posts, Likes, Reposts for own profile; Posts, Reposts for others
edge_cases: Only profile image URL is editable via this route. Full profile editing (name, username, bio) is done through /setting/profile-setting.
Uploading for non-existent user returns 404.
Client-side uses Cropper.js to crop to 300x300 before uploading.

settings.description: Full account settings management across 7 sections with save/cancel actions.

routes.GET /setting/<string:section>: Valid sections: profile-info, account-info, change-password, privacy-setting, notifications-setting, support, delete-account. Invalid sections redirect to profile-info.
routes.POST /setting/profile-setting: Updates name, username (validates uniqueness and format), contact, bio.
routes.POST /setting/account-setting: Updates email (validates format and uniqueness, resets status to UNVERIFIED on change), birthdate, website, about.
routes.POST /setting/password-setting: Verifies current password, checks new==confirm, updates hash.
routes.POST /setting/privacy-settings: Updates private_account, show_birthdate, show_bio, show_status in user settings JSON.
routes.POST /setting/notifications-settings: Updates email_notifications, push_notifications, new_followers, mentions, likes_comments, reposts in user settings JSON.
routes.GET /setting/download-account-data/<int:: Returns ZIP file containing ChatFlick_Data.json with account info, posts, and settings. Requires id == current_user.id.
routes.POST /setting/create-support-request: Creates Support record with category and message.
routes.POST /danger-zone/<int:st>: st=1 deactivates account (status='DEACTIVED', logout). st=2 permanently deletes account and all cascade data (logout).

user_settings_model.storage: user.setting is a MutableDict JSON column. Uses get_setting(section, key, default) and set_setting(section, key, value) methods.
user_settings_model.set_setting_behavior: Values equal to the default are deleted from the stored dict to save space. Only overrides are stored.

edge_cases: Changing email resets account status to UNVERIFIED (user must re-verify).
Username validation: 3-20 chars, letters/numbers/underscores only, case-insensitive (stored lowercase).
danger-zone requires username confirmation on the frontend before submitting.
Settings page uses flash messages rendered as toast notifications via setting-flash-data script tag.

notifications.description: In-app notification inbox and Firebase push notification delivery.

routes.POST /save-token: Saves Firebase FCM token to current_user.fb_auth_token.
routes.GET /notifications: Returns rendered notifications.html with 15 most recent notifications for current_user, ordered by created_at DESC.
routes.GET /notifications/mark_read/<int:user_i: Marks all notifications for user_id as is_read=True. Requires user_id == current_user.id.
routes.GET /check-notifications/<int:user_id>: Returns {unread_count} for user_id. Requires user_id == current_user.id.
routes.POST /send-notification-route/<int:state: state=2 broadcasts to all followers of current_user. state=3 sends to single recipient. push='true' triggers Firebase push delivery.

model.Notification: id, recipient_id (FK), sender_id (FK nullable), title (255), message, type (50), identifier (int nullable), is_read (bool, default False), created_at (DateTime naive UTC).

notification_types_used: new_post — broadcast to followers when user posts
new_follower — sent to followed user when someone follows
post_like — sent to post owner on like
post_retweet — sent to post owner on repost
new_comment — sent to post owner on comment
mention_post — sent to mentioned user in a post
mention_comment — sent to mentioned user in a comment
profile_update — broadcast to followers on profile pic update

services.NotificationService.update_or_create: Deduplicates by (type, identifier, recipient_id). Updates created_at and message if found. Used for likes, reposts, follows (avoids spam).
services.NotificationService.create_notification: Always creates new record. Used rarely.
services.NotificationService.send_mention_notific: Creates new Notification per mention — no deduplication. Skips self-mentions.
services.send_notification (push_service): Initializes Firebase if not already done. Gets fb_auth_token from notification.recipient. Sends FCM Message. Returns bool.

notifications.milestone_logic: Milestone notifications are triggered client-side in home.js and mobile-home.js based on the returned count values. They are NOT triggered server-side.
edge_cases: Mention notifications do not deduplicate — repeated mentions create repeated entries.
Push delivery silently fails if Firebase not initialized or FCM token missing.
Follower broadcast (state=2) skips sender (recipient_id == sender_id check).
Self-notification prevention only in state=3 single-recipient route.
Notification created_at is stored as naive UTC (tzinfo stripped).
check-notifications and mark_read both verify user_id == current_user.id.
Notifications page shows sender.profile_image_url, sender.name, title, message, relative timestamp.

ai_assistant.description: Groq-powered AI chat with authenticated per-user short-term memory and a public stateless endpoint.

routes.GET /Manzoid-AI: Renders AI.html chat page. Requires login. @verified_user decorator is commented out.
routes.POST /Manzoid-AI: Authenticated chat. Requires {message, user_id} in JSON body. message limit 130 chars. Uses per-user conversation history.
routes.POST /api/ai/chat: Public stateless endpoint. Requires {message}. message limit 100 chars. No auth required.

services.AIService.chat: Maintains user_history dict keyed by user_id. Keeps last MAX_HISTORY=2 exchanges. Builds context_prompt string. Uses llama-3.1-8b-instant with max_tokens=180.

edge_cases: Authenticated route requires user_id in payload but does NOT verify it matches current_user.id — any authenticated user can pass any user_id to read/write another user's history.
user_history is stored in process memory (module-level dict). Resets on server restart.
AI page loads inside div3 via AJAX in home.js using $('.div3').load('/Manzoid-AI') — the rendered HTML is injected, not the full page.
Mobile app reconstructs the AI chat UI in JavaScript rather than loading the template.
@verified_user and @pro_user decorators are present in import but commented out on the route.
Returns 503-style JSON error when Groq API is unavailable.

admin.description: Full admin dashboard at /admin. Protected by @login_required + @verified_user + @only_admin (username='hammad').

routes.POST /admin/api/support/<id>/reply: Saves admin_reply text to support request.
routes.POST /admin/api/support/<id>/answer: Sets support status to 'ANSWERED', optionally saves reply.
routes.POST /admin/api/users/<id>/verify: Sets user status to 'VERIFIED' if currently UNVERIFIED. Blocked users must be unblocked first.
routes.POST /admin/api/users/<id>/approve-pro: Sets user status to 'PRO'. Blocked users cannot receive Pro.
routes.POST /admin/api/users/<id>/warning: Increments warnings counter in user settings. Auto-blocks at warnings >= 3.
routes.POST /admin/api/users/<id>/block: Sets status to 'BLOCKED'. Saves previous status to settings.
routes.POST /admin/api/users/<id>/unblock: Restores previous_status from settings. Prevents restoring to 'BLOCKED'.

services.AdminService.dashboard_data: Fetches all support requests, reports, and users ordered by id DESC.
services.AdminService._remember_previous_status: Saves user.status to account-info.previous_status before blocking.
services.AdminService._serialize_user: Returns {id, dbId, name, email, verified, pendingPro, pro, status, rawStatus, warnings, profileUrl}. verified=True if status in {VERIFIED, PENDING_PRO, PRO}.
services.AdminService._serialize_support_request: Formats id as 'SUP-XXXX', userId as 'USR-XXXX'. Includes adminReply.

models.Support: id, category, message, user_id (FK), admin_reply (nullable), created_at, updated_at (nullable), status (default='PENDING').

admin.frontend: admin.js — SPA with Bootstrap modals. Sections: dashboard (metrics), support requests (table + modal), reports (table + modal), users (table + management panel). Uses fetch() API with JSON.

account_discovery.description: Account search and suggestions for the explore tab.

behavior.random: Returns 10 random users via ORDER BY RANDOM().
behavior.search: Matches username containing query (case-insensitive). Joins Follow table to count followers. Orders by follower count DESC. If results > 10, random.sample(10) returned.
behavior.following: Returns list of UserData objects this user is following.
behavior.followers: Returns list of UserData objects following this user.

account_discovery.template: exploreAccounts.html — shows profile image, name, username, follower count, Follow/Following button. Inline script handles follow/unfollow AJAX and sends notifications.

mobile.description: Dedicated mobile UI served at /mobile-home. Auto-redirected from /home when mobile User-Agent detected.
mobile.template: mobile-home.html — bottom nav (Home, Search, More, AI, Profile), accounts-row stories strip, feed-post section, fullpost overlay (bottom sheet).
mobile.script: mobile-home.js — mirrors all home.js functionality. showHome(), showProfile(), showSearch(), showNotifications(), showAi(), showFullPost() (bottom sheet overlay). Uses ajaxWithAbort() for request management.
mobile.ai_on_mobile: AI UI is constructed entirely in JavaScript (not loaded from template). sendMessage() posts to /Manzoid-AI.
mobile.more_menu: Grid overlay above bottom nav with: Settings, About us, Terms, Liked posts, Logout, Switch account, Help.

UserData.fields: id, name, username (unique), profile_image_url (nullable), email (unique), contact (String nullable), password (hashed), fb_auth_token (nullable), liked_posts (MutableList JSON), birth_date (Date nullable), reposted_posts (MutableList JSON), status (String, default='UNVERIFIED'), setting (MutableDict JSON)
UserData.relationships: posts (cascade delete), comments (cascade delete), support (cascade delete), reports (cascade delete), followers (Follow.following_id), following (Follow.follower_id), received_notifications (cascade delete), sent_notifications (cascade delete)
UserData.methods: get_setting(section, key, default=None) — reads from self.setting with DEFAULT_USER_SETTINGS fallback. set_setting(section, key, value) — only stores if value differs from default. Deletes empty nested dicts.

data_model.Post: id, content, user_id, hashtags (JSON), mentions (JSON), timestamp (DateTime UTC), likes, comments, reposts, shares. Cascade deletes Comments.
data_model.Comments: id, content, post_id, user_id, mentions (JSON), likes. No timestamp.
data_model.Follow: id, follower_id, following_id. Both FK to user_data.
data_model.Notification: id, recipient_id, sender_id (nullable), title, message, type, identifier (int nullable), is_read, created_at.
data_model.Support: id, category, message, user_id, admin_reply, created_at, updated_at, status.

utilities.mentions_parser: Extracts @username tokens from content. Queries UserData for matching usernames. Returns list of {user_id, username} dicts.
utilities.validate_username: 3-20 chars, only letters/numbers/underscores, case-insensitive (lowercased). Spaces replaced with underscores.
utilities.build_unique_username: Strips non-alphanumeric chars, lowercases, appends incrementing counter if username already exists.
utilities.PostService._normalize_timestamp: Adds UTC tzinfo to naive timestamps before timedelta comparison.

static_pages./: Landing page (index.html) with Google sign-in, Apple sign-in (placeholder), Create Account, and Login buttons.
static_pages./about: About page (about.html) — mission, features, tech stack, developer bio (M. Hammad Ashraf), development timeline, vision, contact.
static_pages./privacy-policy: Full privacy policy (privacyPolicy.html) including data collected table, authentication, posts, notifications, AI assistant, third parties, pricing policy, user rights, security, children policy, changes.
static_pages./terms-of-service: Full terms of service (termsOfService.html) — 17 sections covering acceptance, eligibility, accounts, content rules, prohibited conduct, engagement, AI, notifications, moderation, membership tiers, IP, third parties, termination, disclaimers, liability, changes, contact.
static_pages./pricing: Pricing page (pricing.html) — Free/$0, Pro/$12/mo (featured), Enterprise/$49/mo cards with monthly/yearly toggle (20% discount). FAQs section. No backend billing.

client_scripts.home.js: Desktop SPA logic. ajaxWithAbort() for AbortController-based request management. Functions: showProfile(), showPosts(), showFullPost(), submitComment(), sendNotification(), prependPost(), exploreAccounts(), checkForNotifications(), markAsRead(), uploadProfileImage(). Handles all post actions (like, repost, share, edit, delete) via event delegation.
client_scripts.mobile-home.js: Mobile SPA equivalent of home.js. Additional functions: showHome(), showSearch(), showAi(), showLikedPosts(), showNotifications(), toggleMoreMenu(), resetOverlay() for bottom sheet. Builds AI UI in JS.
client_scripts.setting.js: Settings page logic. saveChanges(), showSettingToast(), showFlashedMessages(), uploadProfileImage() with Cropper.js integration. Handles all settings sections, danger zone confirmation flow, report/support submission.
client_scripts.admin.js: Admin SPA. Loads data from /admin/api/dashboard. Renders support, reports, users tables. User management panel. Modal interactions for support/report details. Filter/search on each section.
client_scripts.signup.js: Real-time username availability check (keydown AJAX to /signup/1). Password strength meter. Email duplicate check (isEmailValid function defined but call commented out).
client_scripts.resetPassword.js: Reset form submit handler (AJAX POST JSON). New password strength meter. Password confirmation validation.

firebase_and_push.config: Initialized from service account JSON file at startup. Candidate paths checked: FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT env var, corexmanzoid-twitter-notify-firebase.json, firebase-service-account.json.
firebase_and_push.client_config: firebase-messaging-sw.js (gitignored) and firebase.js (gitignored) handle service worker registration and FCM token retrieval. Config injected via window.firebaseRuntimeConfig in home.html and mobile-home.html.
firebase_and_push.token_save: POST /save-token saves FCM token to user.fb_auth_token.
firebase_and_push.push_delivery: send_notification() in push_service.py creates messaging.Message with notification body and data payload {type, identifier, sender_id}.

cloudinary_config.configure_cloudinary: Called at import time in cloudinary_config.py (eager init) and again in CloudinaryService.upload_profile_picture (redundant but safe).
cloudinary_config.note: configure_cloudinary() is called at module import which may cause startup errors if env vars are missing.

app_factory.create_app: Loads .env, creates Flask app, sets SECRET_KEY (hardcoded in code — should be from env), sets SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI (SQLite default, PostgreSQL env var commented out), calls db.create_all() in app context.
app_factory.context_processor: inject_runtime_config injects firebase_enabled, firebase_status, fcm_vapid_key, firebase_api_key, current_time (UTC), current_user into all templates.

known_bugs_and_issues: SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in __init__.py ('8BYkEfBA6O6donzWlSihBXox7C0sKR6b') — should be loaded from environment variable.
Google OAuth creates users with status='VERIFIED' (title-case) but @verified_user requires 'VERIFIED' (uppercase) — Google users cannot access verified-only features.
Duplicate follow rows are possible (no unique constraint on (follower_id, following_id)).
AI route does not verify that user_id in request body matches current_user.id — session hijacking of conversation history is possible.
Comment likes have no uniqueness tracking — easily manipulable.
Editing posts does not re-parse hashtags or @mentions.
Backend enforces no character limit on posts or comments.
post_action_service.py does not defensively handle missing Post/Comment before mutation — can cause AttributeError.
setting.js readFlashedMessages has a variable name bug: catches `err` but references `error` in the catch block.
comments.html template references post.retweets instead of post.reposts for the retweet count in the comment detail view.
isEmailValid() function in signup.js references global `data` variable but the call is commented out.
Logout route uses integer path param for behavior (st=1 vs st=2) rather than query param or session state — fragile API design.
configure_cloudinary() is called eagerly at module import which can cause startup failure if Cloudinary env vars are missing.
delete-account settings section exists but the original system (before danger-zone route) had no backend implementation. Now implemented via /danger-zone/2.
PRO status users cannot pass @verified_user decorator — they cannot follow, comment, upload photos, etc. without the decorator being updated.
